topic: Alsace varietal labelling and dry climate

steps.label: cause
steps.text: Alsace lies in the rain shadow of the Vosges and commonly labels wines by the principal grape variety.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Low rainfall and sunny autumns support ripening, while varietal labelling communicates the aromatic grape identity directly to consumers.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The region is associated with ripe, clearly varietal white wines and labels that foreground grape names.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: the Vosges rain shadow supports a dry ripening season, and varietal labels make grape identity central to Alsace style.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
